behavior_summary: Provides two gameplay interactions for an abacus item: a 'U8P_ABACUS::look' (examine) handler that announces the item to the viewer, and a 'use' handler that counts a specific inventory item (inferred as 'obsidian coin') and reports the total to the user.
ABACUS::look sets item display/info flags for the U8P_ABACUS::look event and sends a short static description string ('abacus ') to the viewing process via Item::bark.

ABACUS::use queries a subset of the actor's equipment/inventory (inferred slot range ~1..6) via Npc::getEquip, iterates the returned list, sums quantities of items matching a specific shape constant (inferred 0x008F), formats the total into a human message with pluralization, and sends it to the actor via Item::bark.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: U8P_ABACUS::ABACUS::look is executed in response to a 'U8P_ABACUS::look' event/area entry; U8P_ABACUS::ABACUS::use runs on the item's use action.
U8P_ABACUS::ABACUS::use performs an enumeration loop over a returned equipment list/pointer (returned by Npc::getEquip), accumulating a running total using repeated Item::getQuantity calls.
Message construction path: numeric total -> numToStr conversion -> concatenation with static text -> conditional plural 's' when total != 1 -> dispatch via Item::bark.
open_questions: The exact mapping of the shape constant 0x008F to 'obsidian coin' is inferred from local string construction; is this mapping defined elsewhere in data/shape tables?
Exact slot indices and semantics passed to Npc::getEquip are inferred as 1..6 from pushed bytes; are the start/end slot parameters documented elsewhere?

Which internal VM operations correspond to the 'set item info' and 'process exclude' writes performed by ABACUS::look (they are recorded as flag writes but the concrete flag names are not present here)?

main_function.name: U8P_ABACUS::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to an examine/U8P_ABACUS::look event for the abacus: set item display/exclusion flags and present the item's short description to the requesting process/player.
entry_conditions: Event 0 triggered when an actor looks at or examines the abacus (enterFastArea / U8P_ABACUS::look event).

function_map.name: U8P_ABACUS::look
function_map.role: Handle the 'U8P_ABACUS::look'/examine interaction for the abacus, set item info flags, and emit a short description to the viewer via Item::bark.

function_map.name: U8P_ABACUS::use
function_map.role: Handle the 'use' interaction: scan actor equipment/inventory for items of a target shape, total their quantities, build a 'You have N <item>(s)' message, and present it to the actor.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Deliver a short text message from an item/process to a target process/player.

function_map.name: Npc::getEquip
function_map.role: Return a pointer/list representing a subset of an actor's equipment/inventory for iteration.

function_map.name: Item::getQuantity
function_map.role: Return the stack/quantity value for a particular item instance.

function_map.name: U8P_ABACUS::numToStr
function_map.role: Convert a numeric value (uword) to its decimal string representation for message construction.

internal_calls.purpose: Emit the static item description string ('abacus ') to the viewing process.

external_calls.purpose: Request a pointer/list of equipped/held items across a slot range to iterate and inspect.
Obtain the numeric count/stack size for each candidate item to add to the running total.
Convert the final numeric total into text for concatenation into the 'You have N ...' message.
Deliver the constructed inventory-count message to the actor who used the abacus.

writes.name: item info / process exclude (ambient look state)
writes.likely_effect: Mark the item with display/behavior flags for the U8P_ABACUS::look event and prevent certain processes from running concurrently for this item/event.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides two gameplay interactions for an abacus item: a 'U8P_ABACUS::look' (examine) handler that announces the item to the viewer, and a 'use' handler that counts a specific inventory item (inferred as 'obsidian coin') and reports the total to the user.
